mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of monthly cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields and forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS spread fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, with positive returns occurring in greater than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in greater than 55% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real gross domestic product falls below 12500 billion chained 2017 dollars, aggregate demand weakness triggers earnings downgrade cycles, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values below 12500 are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized supply and labor market recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in INDPRO and PAYEMS yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of monthly instances.
